evals.prompt: Review the proposed lakehouse design in evals/files/clickstream_pipeline_design.md before the team builds it. Deliver outputs/review.md with severity-ranked findings — each naming the failure mode, citing the design section as evidence, and giving the corrected form — plus a corrected outputs/design.md for the storage, ingestion, retention, and PII sections.
evals.expected_output: Review naming bare-Parquet, user_id over-partitioning, non-idempotent blind append, VACUUM below time-travel retention, and query-side PII masking; corrected design uses Delta/Iceberg, date partitioning or liquid clustering, idempotent MERGE/dedup keyed on a stable key, safe retention, and catalog-level masking at silver.
expectations: Flags bare Parquet and requires Delta Lake or Iceberg for ACID, schema evolution, and time travel
Flags partitioning by high-cardinality user_id as a small-files problem; corrected design partitions on date or uses liquid clustering / hidden partitioning
Flags blind append on consumer restart as non-idempotent duplicate ingestion; corrected design dedups or MERGEs on a stable event key so replays converge
Flags the planned VACUUM RETAIN 24 HOURS as breaking the reproducibility finance depends on — retention must never undercut the time-travel window backing monthly-close reruns
Flags per-dashboard masking of email/ip_address; corrected design masks or tokenizes PII at the silver layer via catalog policies (row filters / column masks), not in ad-hoc queries
Flags the daily full scan of clean/ and prescribes partition pruning or an incremental gold aggregate

evals.prompt: Design and implement the bronze-to-silver CDC pipeline for evals/files/customers_cdc_contract.md. Deliver outputs/silver_customers.py (PySpark or engine-neutral pseudocode is acceptable, but MERGE logic must be concrete), outputs/quality_checks.md deciding drop/quarantine/fail per contract check, and outputs/runbook.md documenting the 3-day replay procedure and how deletes are handled.
evals.expected_output: Medallion pipeline with idempotent MERGE keyed on customer_id ordered by lsn, tombstoned deletes, explicit per-check violation actions, and a partition-level replay runbook.
expectations: Bronze is append-only immutable landing of the raw Debezium envelope; silver is derived and reproducible from bronze
Dedup and MERGE order changes by lsn (not updated_at alone), matching the contract's total-order semantics
MERGE is idempotent on customer_id: re-running any window converges to the same silver state
Deletes tombstone the silver row (is_deleted flag) rather than physically deleting, with bronze history retained
quality_checks.md assigns an explicit action (drop, quarantine, or fail the run) to each of the four contract checks with a rationale
PII handling puts email masking at the silver boundary via catalog-level policy tied to the analyst_pii role, not in consumer queries
runbook.md documents deterministic partition-level replay of a 3-day bronze window

evals.prompt: Review the ML training-data builder in evals/files/churn_training_set.py for data-correctness problems that would silently corrupt the churn model. Deliver outputs/review.md naming each failure mode with line-level evidence, and outputs/fixed_training_set.py correcting them.
evals.expected_output: Review identifying point-in-time leakage from the all-history feature join, training/serving skew from duplicated feature definitions, missing training-data versioning, and unversioned embeddings; fix uses as-of joins bounded by observation_date and pins snapshots/model versions.
expectations: Identifies point-in-time leakage: features aggregate ALL order history, including events after each label's observation_date, leaking the future into training
Fix computes features with an as-of join or a window bounded by observation_date per label row

Identifies training/serving skew risk: serving_feature_sql duplicates the feature definitions as a separately maintained SQL copy; recommends a single shared feature definition (feature store) serving both offline and online
Identifies missing reproducibility: no table snapshot/version or feature-code version is recorded, so a model run cannot be reconstructed
Identifies that the embeddings are unversioned: neither the embedding model nor the source snapshot is pinned, so re-runs silently change the vector space
Flags the full overwrite rebuild of ticket_embeddings and recommends keeping the vector data in sync with source updates via the same CDC/incremental path as other derived tables
review.md names the failure modes explicitly (leakage, skew, reproducibility) with line references rather than generic style notes
